ogTitle: Shelly Beach vs Shirbourne: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Shelly Beach and Shirbourne.
heading: Shelly Beach vs Shirbourne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Shelly Beach, Queensland and Shirbourne, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shelly Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Shirbourne (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Shelly Beach or Shirbourne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Shelly Beach has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Shirbourne scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Shelly Beach scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Shirbourne scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Shelly Beach or Shirbourne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shelly Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.68% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
